signals.raw: **Fed rate cut (third consecutive, 0.25%)**: Powell described the economy as **"very unusual"** with high tariff-driven goods inflation (peaking Q1 2026, absent new tariffs), weakening labor market (official jobs data overstated by ~60k/month), and neutral policy stance at "high end" to balance mandates—no further cuts signaled.[1]
